name: Banana, Walnut, and Chocolate Chip Fritters
recipeInstructions:
Equipment: Measuring cup, measuring spoons, cook's knife, cutting board, 3 stainless steel bowls (1 large), slotted spoon, whisk, rubber spatula, deep fryer or heavy-gauge 4-qt saucepan, candy/deep frying thermometer, #50 (1 1/2 tbsp) ice-cream scoop, tongs, baking sheet, paper towels.
1. In a large stainless steel bowl, combine the flour, light brown sugar, baking powder, cornstarch, salt, and cinnamon.
2. Peel the bananas. Smash them to a rough-textured consistency in a stainless steel bowl using a slotted spoon (they should yield about 1 cup).
3. In another stainless steel bowl whisk together the beer, egg, and lemon juice.
4. Pour the liquid mixture over the bananas, and use the slotted spoon to combine thoroughly.
5. Stir in the chopped walnuts, cinnamon, chocolate chips, and confectioners' sugar until evenly distributed.
6. Heat the oil in a deep fryer or heavy-gauge saucepan to 375°F (190°C).
7. Using an ice-cream scoop, drop spoonfuls of batter into the hot oil, turning off the heat source when frying.
8. Fry the fritters until golden brown on both sides, about 3 minutes per side.
9. Remove the fritters with tongs and place them on paper towels to drain excess oil.
10. Serve immediately while hot.
